title: NEET UG exam 2026 rescheduled for June 21 after paper leak row
content: The National Testing Agency has announced a re-examination for NEET (UG) 2026 on Sunday, June 21, following the cancellation of the May 3 exam due to alleged irregularities. Investigators uncovered evidence of a "guess paper" with numerous questions closely matching the actual exam, prompting a CBI probe into the matter.

title: NIA reveals Pakistan's plan to push 'Ghazwa-e-Hind' through 'doctor-terror' module
content: NIA's chargesheet reveals Ansar Ghazwat-ul-Hind (AGuH) has evolved from a forest-based group to an urban threat, orchestrated by Pakistan-based handlers. This shift, moving from visual propaganda to a tech-driven network, aims to link Kashmiri militancy with global jihadist goals, bypassing traditional structures through encrypted platforms.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: PBKS slump to 43.8% as GT and RCB tighten playoff grip
content: With playoffs in sight for some and out of reach for others, the IPL race intensifies. Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bangalore are nearing qualification, while Sunrisers Hyderabad are strong contenders. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold even chances for the top four, as Punjab Kings' hopes dwindle. Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals face slim prospects.

title: 'Elections over, vasooli shuru': Oppn hits out at PM over fuel hike with 'mehangayi man' jab
content: Fuel prices have increased across India. Petrol and diesel rates have gone up by Rs 3 per litre. Opposition parties are criticising the Modi government. They state that elections are over and price recovery has begun. This hike comes amid global energy price surges. India had previously absorbed these pressures. State-owned oil companies are now adjusting prices.

title: As layoffs cross 1 lakh across tech industry, here are AI-proof jobs for engineers
content: Tech layoffs have surged past 1 lakh in 2026, impacting major firms like LinkedIn and Meta, even as companies invest heavily in AI. While mid-level and entry-level roles are cut, demand is rising for experienced engineers skilled in AI operations, maintenance, and integration. Soft skills and a proven AI track record are now crucial for securing these sought-after positions.

title: Fuel prices up: Petrol, diesel hiked by Rs 3/litre amid global crude surge
content: The Centre has repeatedly asserted that there is no fuel shortage in the country and no plan to introduce rationing of petrol, diesel or LPG despite disruptions in global energy shipments linked to the Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

title: 'Compromised PM': Rahul Gandhi's fresh swipe at PM Modi over India-US trade deal
content: Rahul Gandhi has accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of striking a "deal for Adani's release" with the US, rather than a genuine trade agreement. The Congress party echoed these sentiments, labeling the pact "one-sided" and alleging it aims to close fraud cases against Gautam Adani in America, suggesting Modi is compromised and subservient to US demands.

title: ‘Dispute over money’: Accused in Delhi bus gangrape case tells cops; what we know so far
content: Delhi Police have apprehended the driver and conductor of a private sleeper bus. They are accused of raping a 30-year-old woman in Nangloi three days ago. The woman stated she boarded the bus late at night. The accused allegedly drove the vehicle to a secluded area and assaulted her. The accused claim a monetary dispute was involved. Investigations are ongoing.

title: 'While AI writes 90% of company code, we hired a lot more...': Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao
content: Anthropic's CFO reveals AI now writes 90% of the company's code, yet hiring is at an all-time high. AI acts as a productivity accelerant, automating routine tasks and allowing human employees to focus on oversight, judgment, and strategy.

title: PM Modi leaves for UAE - what's on his five-nation tour agenda
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi begins a significant five-nation tour. He will visit the United Arab Emirates, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, and Italy. The visits aim to boost India's engagement with Europe and the Gulf region. Discussions will focus on trade, investment, energy, and technology. This tour underscores India's growing global partnerships.

title: 117 killed as storms lash UP with cyclone-like 130kmph winds
content: A severe thunderstorm and squall ravaged Uttar Pradesh on Wednesday, claiming 117 lives and injuring 79. Violent storms, collapsing structures, and lightning strikes caused widespread destruction, with Prayagraj being the worst-hit district. High wind speeds, reaching nearly cyclonic levels, uprooted trees and damaged hundreds of houses and livestock.

title: Elon Musk says: Read this book and give it to all your friends
content: Elon Musk has lauded Gad Saad's new book, 'Suicidal Empathy: Dying to Be Kind,' calling it vital for civilization's survival. The book argues that excessive, irrational empathy in Western culture prioritizes offenders and feelings over reason and societal stability, leading to a dangerous decline. Musk urges everyone to read and share this critical work.

title: ‘Xi would also like to see the Hormuz Strait open’: Trump on US-China talks
content: US President Trump revealed Chinese President Xi Jinping desires the Strait of Hormuz reopened to stabilize global energy markets. Both leaders reportedly agreed on the necessity of this crucial shipping route's resumption. Discussions also touched upon potential increased Chinese purchases of American oil and agricultural products, alongside ongoing tensions regarding Taiwan.

title: Check latest petrol, diesel rates in your city and state after Rs 3 increase
content: Fuel prices have surged across India, with petrol now exceeding Rs 110 per litre in several states and diesel nearing Rs 100 in many regions. This hike, averaging Rs 3 per litre, follows sustained pressure from elevated global crude oil prices, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis.

title: From doctrine to dominance: how integrated systems shaped Operation Sindoor
content: Outgoing CDS General Anil Chauhan highlighted India's success in Operation Sindoor, attributing it to integrated systems and superior situational awareness, leading to a non-kinetic victory. He stressed the need for multi-domain warfare readiness and cultural shifts for effective theatre commands, acknowledging a decade-long capability gap in implementation.

title: 'Torturous for boxers': Drained Nikhat Zareen suffers shock trials exit
content: Two-time world champion Nikhat Zareen faced a shocking defeat in boxing trials, missing out on CWG and Asian Games. She revealed the immense physical and mental strain caused by a chaotic two-week preparation, citing administrative tussles between BFI and SAI as a major factor. Despite the setback, Zareen plans to focus on rebuilding her fitness for the LA Olympics.

title: 2 men rape orphaned girls aged 11 and 12 for over a year in Bengaluru, post videos online
content: Two men have been arrested in Bengaluru for sexually abusing two minor girls, recording the acts, and uploading them online for profit. The arrests followed an alert from the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.

title: 'You wouldn't find a guy like him in Hollywood': Trump praises China's Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump praised Chinese President Xi Jinping, calling him a respected leader governing nearly 1.5 billion people and suggesting Hollywood couldn't find someone like him. Trump agreed with Xi's assessment of the US as a declining nation, attributing it to the Biden administration's policies, while highlighting his own administration's economic successes.

title: Groom not the man she met on Instagram, bride calls off wedding in UP
content: A wedding in Shahabad descended into chaos when the bride alleged the groom was not the man she knew online. The man, identified as Devendra Singh Parmar, claimed he was the one the bride had been speaking to. Police have registered an FIR against 10 people for cheating and conspiracy, arresting five, including the groom.

title: 'I don't see that happening': Bumrah on ODI captaincy after MI win
content: Jasprit Bumrah captained Mumbai Indians for the first time in IPL 2026, leading them to a six-wicket victory against Punjab Kings. This leadership role came in the absence of regular captains Hardik Pandya and Suryakumar Yadav. Bumrah, who has captained India in Tests and T20Is, expressed his enjoyment of the experience and praised the team's execution.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bajaj Broking Research has chosen NMDC, and Jayaswal Neco Industries as the top stock picks for May 15, 2026. The brokerage has shared its target prices and time period to attain it. Let’s take a look:

title: RPI(Athavale) leader stabbed to death at Pune bar
content: Republican Party of India (Athavale)'s Kaamgar Aghadi state executive president Anil Maruti More was brutally stabbed to death by two assailants at a Moshi bar. The murder is believed to be a fallout of personal enmity, with police investigating CCTV footage and other clues to apprehend the suspects.

title: BRICS consensus on West Asia uncertain as Iran, UAE spar at meeting
content: BRICS foreign ministers are struggling to agree on a joint statement concerning West Asia. Tensions flared between the UAE and Iran during the meeting. Iran accused the UAE of direct involvement in aggression. India's External Affairs Minister stressed the importance of consensus for new members. The outcome of the meeting remains uncertain.

title: 'Unfortunately ... ': Tilak Varma's candid Chahal remark after MI beat PBKS
content: Tilak Varma's brilliant unbeaten 75 guided Mumbai Indians to a six-wicket victory over Punjab Kings. Chasing 200, Varma's calculated aggression, including a crucial 22-run 18th over, sealed the win with two balls to spare. This defeat significantly dents PBKS's playoff aspirations.

title: ‘Tough pill to swallow’: Shreyas admits playoff pressure after PBKS’ fifth straight loss
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer expressed disappointment after their fifth consecutive IPL 2026 loss to Mumbai Indians in Dharamsala on Thursday, a defeat that significantly pressures their playoff hopes. Despite posting 200, Tilak Varma's unbeaten 75 steered MI to victory. Punjab Kings now face two must-win games to avoid elimination now.

title: Bangladesh pace sensation bags ICC Player of the Month after dismantling NZ
content: Nahid Rana has been named ICC Men's Player of the Month for April 2026 following an exceptional performance that secured Bangladesh's ODI series victory against New Zealand.  Rana's eight wickets in three matches, including a crucial five-wicket haul, earned him both Player of the Series and this prestigious monthly award.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 57: Ranveer Singh starrer sees 5.4% dip
content: In an exhilarating turn of events, the espionage drama 'Dhurandhar 2', featuring the charismatic Ranveer Singh and the versatile Rakesh Bedi, has successfully amassed an astounding Rs 1,796 crore globally in just 57 days. After making its international debut on OTT platforms on May 14, 2026, fans in India are eagerly anticipating its upcoming digital release.

title: How a 9-year-old found a human ancestor's fossil
content: During an  excursion in South Africa's renowned 'Cradle of Humankind', young Matt Berger, just nine years old, stumbled upon a remarkable fossil. This unexpected find, in the company of his father, the esteemed paleoanthropologist Lee Berger, led to the discovery of a previously unknown ancestor, Australopithecus sediba, dating back almost two million years.

title: Manipur killings trigger retaliatory abductions, 38 people held hostage
content: Manipur faces a critical crisis as tensions rise following the tragic deaths of three church leaders and a civilian, leading to a wave of kidnappings and paralyzing shutdowns in the hilly regions. With reports of over 38 hostages taken by conflicting factions, authorities are urgently working to negotiate their freedom.

title: Compromised PM didn't sign trade deal with U.S., but a bargain for Adani's release: Rahul
content: Hopelessly one-sided Indo-U.S. trade deal that was really a steal by the U.S., says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh in a post on X.

title: How Tagore’s songs were ahead of their time in form and feeling
content: Rabindrasangeet, a unique genre of Indian music, bridges classical rigour with folk spontaneity and Western influences, while anticipating modern concerns.

title: PM Modi UAE visit LIVE: PM Modi embarks on six-day visit to UAE, 4 European nations
content: India and the UAE are expected to firm up two significant agreements to boost cooperation in areas of LPG and strategic petroleum reserves

title: Former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan slams Centre for hiking fuel prices
content: He said the Centre had used the rise of crude oil prices owing to the crisis in West Asia to fleece ordinary people, who would feel the bite of the fuel price hike acutely

title: Semi-high-speed train project to turn Gujarat into manufacturing and logistics hub: Amit Shah
content: The Home Minister said that the project will provide direct connectivity to the Dholera International Airport and the Lothal National Maritime Heritage Complex

title: Kerala politics LIVE: UDF leadership meeting to decide next Kerala Cabinet underway
content: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala said that the CM has the prerogative to decide the composition of the Cabinet

title: National Testing Agency announces NEET-UG re-exam on June 21
content: On May 12, the NTA had announced the cancellation of NEET-UG and said the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) would carry out a comprehensive inquiry into the alleged irregularities linked to the exam

title: UDF leadership meeting to lay out the contours of the next Kerala cabinet gets underway
content: The UDF is seeking to turn the page on the uncertainty that gripped the alliance following the Congress’s ten-day delay in choosing its Chief Ministerial candidate by fast-tracking the government formation process.

title: ‘Union Government should include enhanced quota under 9th schedule’
content: Karnataka’s Public Works Minister Satish Jarkiholi, who belongs to the Nayaka community, a scheduled tribe, spoke to The Hindu

title: Supreme Court summons Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh officials over ‘casual approach’ to Chambal illegal sand mining
content: The sanctuary, spread across Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh, is India’s first and only tri-State riverine protected area

title: Goa Congress leader Ketan Bhatikar dies of snakebite
content: The Goa Pradesh Congress Committee paid tribute to its party leader and extended condolences to the bereaved family, as well as his friends and supporters

title: Fixing gadgets in vehicles of no use without monitoring mechanism: Karnataka State Travel Operators’ Association
content: Without proper monitoring centres, many of the electronic safety gadgets risk becoming only compliance formalities, according to Karnataka State Travel Operators’ Association President Radhakrishna Holla

title: A detente emerges in Congress as Ramesh Chennithala belatedly backs Chief Minister-designate V.D. Satheesan
content: Mr Chennithala also appeared to dispel reports that he would eschew joining Mr Satheesan’s cabinet. When asked whether he would join the government as a Minister, Mr Chennithala said, “Forming the cabinet is the Chief Minister’s prerogative”

title: Inspired by Vijay’s ‘Thamizhan’, Chennai man donates ₹22,674 to CM Relief Fund
content: Chennai-based Arun Loganathan, inspired by a Tamil movie starring Vijay, has contributed towards ‘fulfilling his individual debt responsibility’

title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked by ₹3 across all variants
content: The latest price hikes extend to both the regular variants of petrol and diesel alongside the premium and high-octane products

title: Death toll in explosion at firecracker factory in M.P.'s Dewas rises to five
content: Officials said Anil Malviya, the licensed owner of the firecracker factory operating in the Tonk Kalan area has been arrested under the stringent provisions of the National Security Act (NSA)

title: Unorganised labourers, trade unions in Rajasthan seek minimum wage revision
content: Activists said the current wage rates were inadequate for meeting the basic necessities of food, healthcare, education, rent and transportation. Since the workers are unable to cover minimum expenses for their families

title: Congress leader and former Jharkhand Minister, Alamgir Alam, walks out of jail
content: On May 15, 2024, the Enforcement Directorate arrested Mr. Alamin in the money laundering case and seized money worth ₹32.20 crore from the premises linked to his associates

title: MAHE and Griffith University expand India-Australia academic partnership with dual Ph.D programme
content: This initiative aims at addressing the growing need for globally connected and interdisciplinary doctoral training

title: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar hits out at ‘unilateral’ sanctions days before expiry of U.S.’ Russian oil waiver
content: U.S.’ second waiver on purchase of Russian crude oil is set to expire May 16; India’s oil imports from Moscow have surged to about 1.96 million barrels per day in May since the start of the month

title: Police on alert as racketeers forge papers for organ trade
content: A State-wide raid held on May 8 exposed an extensive organ trade racket in which forged documents were used to bypass laws that plug loopholes in organ donation. Five cases were registered, nine people arrested, and fabricated documents seized. The illegal trade thrives on patients’ desperate need for transplant and the monetary benefits that come the donors’ way.

title: Granite quarrying ‘kicking up dust’
content: The villages of Kuppam, Bangarupalem and Palamaner in Andhra Pradesh’s Chittoor district have been witnessing year-round granite mining activities. Although a regulatory system is in place, many operators resort to illegal quarrying which has repeatedly come under the scrutiny of the National Green Tribunal. The issue even drew political attention when TDP chief N. Chandrababu Naidu inspected quarries a few years ago, and expressed concern over unchecked mining, environmental damage and revenue loss, reports K. Umashanker

title: Destroying Sanatana does not mean people should not go to temples, says Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: Mr. Udhayanidhi said equal rights should be given to everyone, not only in temples but in society as a whole

title: Trinamool party office, clock tower demolished in West Bengal
content: A Kolkata Municipal Corporation team demolished a green clock tower in south Kolkata’s Garia area; in North 24 Parganas’ Naihati area, an illegal Trinamool party office was razed; authorities said the party office was constructed on land belonging to the Railways.

title: Ladakh’s high-altitude Changthang region gets irrigation canal at 14,000 feet
content: Ladakh Lieutenant Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena said the project would greatly benefit the people of Changthang by strengthening irrigation infrastructure, improving agricultural productivity and supporting the livelihoods of farmers and pastoral communities

title: Rich harvest caught in procurement tangle
content: As Telangana’s rabi season enters a crucial phase, farmers are grappling with delayed crop procurement, shortage of transport vehicles and inadequate storage facilities. With untimely rains threatening farm produce lying under the open sky, many cultivators fear mounting losses and fresh debt despite a record harvest. Travelling through farming villages and purchase centres, P. Sridhar, K. Shiva Shanker, P. Laxma Reddy and B. Chandrashekhar capture the growing distress unfolding across the countryside

title: U.S. to drop graft charges against Gautam Adani: NY Times
content: Gautam Adani was alleged in November 2024 to have agreed to pay more than $250 million in bribes to Indian officials for lucrative solar-energy supply contracts

title: Work from home advisory, ‘Metro Day’, online classes: Delhi announces austerity measures
content: Drive includes staggered office timings, ban on official foreign visits for one year; govt. will not purchase new petrol, diesel, CNG, or hybrid vehicles for 6 months, says CM; Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs pitches for ‘prudent mobility’

title: Kerala politics highlights: Congress names V.D. Satheesan as Chief Minister; BJP points at 'IUML pressure'
content: Congress leaders and newly elected MLAs extended their greetings to V.D. Satheesan soon after the AICC announced him as the CM of Kerala

title: West Bengal government mandates singing Vande Mataram in all State-run schools during morning assembly
content: The notification reserves the order by the TMC in November 2025 where the government made it mandatory for all schools run and aided by the State Government to sing the State Song, Banglar Mati, Banglar Jol, during their morning assembly

title: Delhi court orders OpIndia to remove articles on Swati Chaturvedi
content: The court held that the continued circulation of the pieces could cause “serious and irreparable injury” to her professional reputation while the defamation suit remains pending.

title: Ten new MLCs sworn in at Maharashtra Vidhan Bhavan
content: During the ceremony, the Legislative Council chairperson Ram Shinde administered the oath to the 10 members

title: AAP, Congress raises concern over SIR in Punjab ahead of 2027 Assembly polls
content: Haryana Chief Electoral Officer A. Sreenivas said under the third phase of the SIR campaign, July 1, 2026, has been fixed as the qualifying date for State

title: BJP MLA Rathindra Bose to be party's candidate for Speaker's post
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced the decision on May 14 and urged Opposition parties to unanimously elect him as the Speaker.

title: 806 locals held, 97 properties attached, 41 demolished in anti-drug drive in J&K in 30 days: DGP Nalin Prabhat
content: He said 41 properties worth nearly ₹15 crore, “identified as proceeds of drug trafficking”, were demolished after completion of legal formalities

title: CM Fadnavis’ bike ride draws Opposition ire
content: Maharashtra CM Fadnavis’ bike ride sparks opposition criticism amid austerity calls, questioning the government’s sincerity and measures post-election

title: ED arrests Kolkata Police DC Shantanu Sinha Biswas in money laundering case
content: ED issued a lookout notice for Shantanu Biswas for ignoring summons; he was viewed as close to the former TMC government, and Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari had previously made allegations against police officials

title: Woman gang-raped inside bus in Delhi’s Rani Bagh, two arrested
content: The two accused have been arrested in connection with the incident that took place on May 11, the sources said

title: One dead, several injured in landslide in Jamuria coal mines in West Bengal; many feared missing
content: It is reported that, during the blasting process of mining, there was “air blast” in section 27 of the Kunustoria coal mines, leading to collapse of multiple pillars and trigging a landslide. Workers alleged lack of supervision and safety measures

title: Efforts on to release Kuki, Naga people held hostage in Manipur, say Ministers
content: State Home Minister says the government is working with civil society organisations and political leaders to get the hostages released; hill districts of the State remain tense after the May 13 ambushes

title: NC’s Farooq Abdullah backs Hosabale and Naravane’s stand on talks with Pakistan
content: Mr. Hosabale and Mr. Naravane, in two separate statements, had stressed on “keeping the window of dialogue with Pakistan open”

title: CBI conducts searches in Reliance Communications case
content: The agency conducted searches at seven premises in Mumbai, Gurugram, and Bengaluru linked to senior executives of the company who served during 2015-2017

title: Unseasonal storms leave 111 dead in Uttar Pradesh
content: At least 72 people injured as heavy rain and thunderstorms wreak havoc across State; 179 livestock losses, damage to 227 houses reported; CM directs officials to aid victims, release compensation

title: Uttarakhand files first FIR on allegations of nikah halala under Uniform Civil Code
content: The complaint filed by a woman against her husband and in-laws also includes dowry, Muslim marriage and other criminal offences

title: Vellore Corporation installs free drinking water points at 20 spots for public during summer
content: Corporation officials said that water supply to free water distribution points will not affect regular water supply to households

title: Chennai emerges as India’s second-largest jewellery leasing market
content: T. Nagar, Anna Nagar, and Velachery continue to remain among the most sought-after retail destinations in Chennai

title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: China Says Trump, Xi Reached 'New Consensuses' On Handling Concerns
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Can PM Modi's UAE Visit Insulate India From Oil Shocks? What To Expect
content: PM Modi's 5-Nation Visit: It's expected that energy will dominate the conversation between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and UAE President.

title: Trump Explains Why He Agreed With Xi Jinping Calling US A "Declining Nation"
content: In a long post on his Truth Social platform, Trump said Xi was "100 per cent correct" in his assessment that the United States was on a decline as the "country suffered immeasurably" due to the Biden administration's policies.

title: Trump Wants Iran's Uranium For "Public Relations", Positive "Will Get It"
content: US President Donald Trump suggested that hunting down Iran's enriched uranium was primarily for political optics, after Israel demanded...

content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

content: Investigators are also probing whether nationals from countries other than Sri Lanka may have been involved.

title: NDTV Exclusive: A Catastrophe Captured By Satellites, Ignored By The World
content: In Darfur, the violence that once mobilised international outrage in the early years of the civil war, has returned in a form many here insist never truly disappeared.

title: Petrol Diesel Prices Hike LIVE: "Shielding Citizens Instead Of Passing Oil Shock," Say Government Sources On Fuel Price Hike
content: Petrol, Diesel Price Hiked India LIVE Updates: The petrol will now cost Rs 97.77 per litre in Delhi, Rs 108.74 per litre in Kolkata, Rs 106.68 in Mumbai and Rs 103.67 per litre in Chennai.

title: With Iran And UAE Present, India's 'Hormuz' Message To BRICS Nations
content: Jaishankar stressed that "safe and unimpeded maritime flows through international waterways, including the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea, remain vital for global economic well-being".

title: Petrol Now Costs Rs 108 In Kolkata: Know Price In Your City
content: Petrol in the national capital Delhi will now cost Rs 97.77 a litre, while diesel will be priced at Rs 99.67.

title: CIA Director Visits Cuba As Energy Crisis Deepens
content: The Central Intelligence Agency, at the heart of the decades-long struggle between the United States and Cuba, confirmed a Cuban government statement about Director John Ratcliffe's visit.

title: US Man Arrested After Video Shows He Threw Rock At Endangered Seal In Hawaii
content: Igor Mykhaylovych Lytvynchuk, 38, from Covington, Washington, was taken into custody near Seattle on Wednesday and charged with harassing a protected marine animal, according to the US attorney's office in Honolulu.

title: Israel To Sue New York Times Over Article On Rape Of Palestinian Detainees
content: Benjamin Netanyahu instructed his legal advisers "to consider the harshest legal action" against the newspaper and journalist who reported the story from the occupied West Bank.

title: At BRICS Meet, Iran Calls For Unity Against "US Bullying"
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi urged BRICS nations to unite against American bullying, arguing that such practices must be consigned to the "dustbin of history."

title: Lavrov gets an exclusive audience with Modi, Araghchi meets Doval
content: lavrov briefed modi on the progress achieved in bilateral cooperation since the meeting of the two leaders in Dec 2025 for the 23rd India-Russia annual summit . he also discussed various regional and global issues of mutual interest, including the situation in Ukraine and West Asia .

title: West Delhi gang rape: Bus traced to Bihar was plying with several challans pending
content: the bus, having a Bihar licence plate, was registered in Gopalganj . it was digitally impounded by transport authorities for violations, including operating without a valid permit, overloading and speeding . police are yet to confirm if the pending challans totalled to Rs 4 lakh .

title: Indian dhow attacked in Omani waters, crew rescued
content: two more ships, carrying LPG, crossed the strife-hit Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday evening and Thursday morning . govt deplored the fact that commercial shipping and civilian mariners continue to be targeted . the attack led to a fire and subsequent sinking of the vessa .
